Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to analyze an insurance claims processing event log to identify cases with performance issues long durations correlate these issues with specific attributes Resource Region Complexity and propose root causes and mitigation strategies

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Data I need to calculate the total duration for each Case ID to determine which ones are slow

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2001 Start 2024 04 01 09 00 End 2024 04 01 10 30 Duration 1 hour 30 minutes 1 5 hours Attributes Resource CSR Jane Adjuster Mike etc Region A Complexity Low

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2002 Start 2024 04 01 09 05 End 2024 04 02 11 00 Duration From Apr 1 09 05 to Apr 2 11 00 That is 1 day plus 1 hour 55 mins about 25 9 hours Attributes Resource CSR Paul Adjuster Lisa etc Region B Complexity Medium

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Note There is a Request Additional Documents event on Apr 1 14 00 and next event Approve Claim on Apr 2 10 00 Gap is overnight weekend Let us assume standard business hours or just look at raw time Raw time is about 26 hours

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2003 Start 2024 04 01 09 10 End 2024 04 03 09 30 Duration From Apr 1 09 10 to Apr 3 09 30 That is 2 days plus 20 mins about 48 hours Attributes Resource CSR Jane Adjuster Mike etc Region A Complexity High

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: Note Multiple Request Additional Documents events 11 00 17 00 Gap between 17 00 Apr 1 and 16 00 Apr 2 is overnight Gap between 16 00 Apr 2 and 09 00 Apr 3 is overnight

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2004 Start 2024 04 01 09 20 End 2024 04 01 10 45 Duration 1 hour 25 minutes Attributes Region B Complexity Low

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2005 Start 2024 04 01 09 25 End 2024 04 04 14 30 Duration From Apr 1 09 25 to Apr 4 14 30 That is 3 days plus 5 hours about 77 hours Attributes Region B Complexity High

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Note Three Request Additional Documents events Apr 1 11 30 Apr 2 17 00 Apr 3 15 00 Huge gaps between these requests

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Identify Performance Issues Fast Cases 2001 1 5 hrs 2004 1 25 hrs Both are Low Complexity Slow Cases 2002 26 hrs Medium Complexity 2003 48 hrs High Complexity 2005 77 hrs High Complexity Conclusion High and Medium complexity cases take significantly longer than Low complexity cases Specifically Case 2005 is the worst offender

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Complexity Low 2001 2004 leads to Fast less than 2 hours Medium 2002 leads to Slower about 26 hours High 2003 2005 leads to Slowest 48 plus hours Correlation Strong positive correlation between Complexity and Duration High complexity triggers Request Additional Documents multiple times

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Region Region A 2001 Low leads to Fast 2003 High leads to Slow 48 hrs Region B 2002 Medium leads to Slow 26 hrs 2004 Low leads to Fast 2005 High leads to Very Slow 77 hrs Correlation Region itself does not seem to be the primary driver Region A has fast and slow Region B has fast medium and very slow

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: However looking closely at Case 2005 Region B High it has more document requests 3 times compared to Case 2003 Region A High 2 times Region B might have a slightly higher burden in High complexity scenarios but Complexity is the stronger factor

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Resource Adjuster Lisa Region B Handles 2002 Medium 2004 Low 2005 High 2004 Low was very fast 2002 Medium had 1 doc request 2005 High had 3 doc requests Adjuster Mike Region A Handles 2001 Low 2003 High 2001 Low was fast 2003 High had 2 doc requests

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Observation The specific resource Adjuster does not seem to be the bottleneck in terms of individual task time but rather the volume of work document requests they generate for High Complexity cases However Adjuster Lisa Region B generated 3 document requests for Case 2005 whereas Adjuster Mike Region A generated 2 for Case 2003 This suggests Region B might be slightly less efficient or stricter in High Complexity cases OR Case 2005 was just inherently harder

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Primary Root Cause Complexity Level High complexity claims trigger iterative Request Additional Documents loops Each loop adds significant time waiting for customer response plus re evaluation

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Secondary Root Cause Iterative Process Region B Adjuster Lisa Case 2005 Region B had 3 requests vs Case 2003 Region A had 2 requests for similar High Complexity This suggests Region B or Adjuster Lisa might have stricter initial evaluation criteria or less efficient communication channels leading to more back and forth

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Tertiary Root Cause Resource Availability Workflow Manager Bill handled the approvals for the slow cases If managers are bottlenecks that is an issue but the data shows the delay happens before approval waiting for docs

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For Complexity Triage claims better Route Low complexity to automated straight through processing

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For Document Requests Improve initial data capture Train adjusters to ask for all necessary docs upfront to reduce loops

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For Region B Adjuster Lisa Investigate if they are more conservative Compare their approval rates or average doc requests against Adjuster Mike

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: SLA Monitoring Set alerts for cases entering the Request Additional Documents loop more than once

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction during drafting Wait I need to be careful not to overstate the Region difference

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Region B High 2005 greater than Region A High 2003 Region B Medium 2002 greater than Region A Low 2001 Region B Low 2004 about equal to Region A Low 2001 So Region B seems to have a slight negative performance impact specifically on High Complexity claims compared to Region A I will mention this as a specific observation but keep Complexity as the dominant factor

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Conclusion The resource Lisa might be less efficient at gathering requirements upfront for High Complexity cases compared to Mike

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Review Does this answer the prompt Yes Identifies slow cases analyzes attributes proposes root causes and mitigations Calculation Check 2001 1 5 hrs 2002 25h 55m 2003 48h 20m 2004 1h 25m 2005 77h 5m Slow cases 2002 2003 2005 Fast cases 2001 2004 Pattern Low Complexity Fast High Medium Slow
